title: AUDIENCE-ASK — Eva: 'Should we tell Papa?' then cheerful sing-song call
prompt: Medium shot of @backyard, soft sunny afternoon, cheerful kid-show comedy lighting. @Eva already CENTER-LEFT in the grass on her knees, naughty playful grin, pressing one finger to her lips in a soft 'shhh' gesture, head turned DIRECTLY toward the camera with a friendly scheming sparkle in her eyes. @Sara already CENTER-RIGHT next to her sister also kneeling in the grass close beside her, both girls still pink-cheeked from happy laughing, both giggling softly. Both girls have exactly two arms and two hands each, all visible. (BEAT 1 — 0-5s) Eva keeps her finger gently to her lips, holds her warm look at the camera with a friend-to-friend conspiratorial twinkle. Eva (to camera, finger to lips, soft warm conspiratorial whisper): "Should we tell Papa?" She holds the friendly look at the camera for two full beats — waiting cheerfully for the kid watching at home to answer out loud. Sara nods solemnly beside her, pressing both hands over her own mouth, beaming. (BEAT 2 — 5-10s) Eva's whisper-grin breaks into a huge cheerful playful smile and her face lights up. She gently cups both hands loosely around her mouth in a comic 'kid-megaphone' shape and lets out a bright sing-song cheerful playful call (warm and silly, NOT scary, NOT angry, NOT a horror-shout). Eva (cheerful sing-song playful call, warm and bright): "Paaa-paaaa! Loooo-ooooook!" Sara giggles helplessly, hands still over her mouth, eyes shining bright. Sara (warm giggling, leaning in to Eva): "He's gonna laugh." Eva (still beaming gently straight at camera, sweet sparkle in her eyes): "Watch this. Watch this." Calm cheerful kid-show energy throughout — no shouting, no scary intensity, no environmental shake.
